items.title: 'Very troubled project': Hospital cost blowout concerns treasurer

children.content: The new Women's and Children's Hospital was priced at $3.2 billion in 2022, before inflation and other pressures sent costs in the construction industry soaring.

articles.title: GST push to follow SA budget, treasurer says

children.content: South Australia's treasurer has foreshadowed a push to secure GST revenue, calling on MPs across the political spectrum to step up their advocacy efforts — but the opposition has questioned why a recent GST windfall is not being used to counter soaring debt.

image.altText: A close-up of a paper booklet that says State Budget 2026-27. In the background a man stands on stage talking at a podium.

articles.title: No surprises but risks aplenty in Tom Koutsantonis's comeback budget

children.content: Some things haven't changed since Labor Treasurer Tom Koutsantonis last handed down a state budget in 2017.

image.altText: A man wearing a navy suit and blue tie sits in a red chair in front of a window holding a booklet that says State Budget 2026-27

articles.title: Are you a winner or loser in this year's SA budget?

children.content: South Australian Treasurer Tom Koutsantonis has unveiled the 2026-27 state budget. Here are the winners and losers.

image.altText: Icon drawing of Australia with state of South Australia highlighted.

gallery.altText: A man in a navy suit sits on a chair inside looking at the camera with a neutral expression.

items.title: Lorna has lived through two world wars, two pandemics and 26 Australian prime ministers

children.content: After a couple of years as the country's oldest woman, Lorna Henstridge is still going strong.

image.altText: An elderly woman holding an oval framed photo of a young child wearing Elizabethan costume

items.title: Future of iconic wine Wolf Blass unclear amid large brand overhaul

children.content: A cloud hangs over the future of the famous Barossa brand as its maker, Treasury Wine Estates, looks to cull more than half of its labels.

image.altText: a red wine label

items.title: Mother creates modest activewear to empower herself and others

children.content: Yasmin Jammoul was sick of wearing oversized men's T-shirts to the gym when she started exercising postpartum, so she created her own clothing line.

gallery.altText: A woman with a dark green hijab and black outfit sitting on a chair smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with a dark green hijab and black outfit sitting on a chair smiling.

items.title: Public servant claims threat of $4m damages bill in golf course fight

children.content: A South Australian public servant taking the state government to court over a controversial redevelopment in the Adelaide Parklands says he will continue to fight the case despite it potentially leading to him losing his house or becoming bankrupt.

image.altText: A man wearing a green shirt standing in front of a line of trees and a dirt path while holding folders

items.title: Body-in-suitcase killer loses bid to reduce sentence for double murder

children.content: The NSW Court of Criminal Appeal dismisses a bid by Daniel Holdom to have his sentence reduced after he pleaded guilty in 2018 to murdering his girlfriend and her two-year-old daughter before dumping the child's body in a suitcase on the side of a remote highway.

image.altText: Khandalyce Kiara Pearce and Karlie Jade Pearce-Stevenson

items.title: Coach defends doctors as Crows players play out game after head clash

children.content: Matthew Nicks defends his medical staff after two Crows were cleared to play out their win against the Cats without a concussion test after a nasty head clash.

items.title: Honouring the man who kept cinema alive in remote outback city

children.content: The Silver City Cinema in remote NSW has stood the test of time for nearly 60 years. Now its new owners want to honour the legend who kept it alive.

image.altText: A black and white photograph of a man dressed up as Rambo, posing with a gun

items.title: Paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy leaves prison after 25 years

children.content: Notorious paedophile ex-magistrate Peter Liddy has taken his first steps of freedom after serving a 25-year prison term for the sexual abuse of four children.

image.altText: A man in a black hat and black jacket walks through a gate with a brick building behind,

items.title: 'Sick and tired': Northern Adelaide hospital nurses walk off job for 24 hours

children.content: Nurses and midwives at the Lyell McEwin Hospital have walked off the job as pay negotiations with the state government continue.

image.altText: A lady in a black t-shirt stands with arms folded defiantly out front of the entrance to Lyell McEwin Hospital

items.title: The US ally getting nuclear-powered submarines with no AUKUS deal

children.content: South Korea is taking a different path to Australia in building nuclear-powered submarines, but its plan still hinges on the United States.

image.altText: Three men in white uniforms and caps salute at the entrance on top of a large grey submarine, with a South Korean flag behind.

items.title: Full Circle launches professional musical theatre company in Adelaide

children.content: Musical theatre typically draws strong audiences in Adelaide where a troupe dedicated to the genre has just launched a professional company that will produce Catch Me If You Can in November.

image.altText: Woman sits on a chair in a garden

items.title: 'Too dangerous to push ahead': Aussie diver leaves Laos cave rescue

children.content: Australian diver Josh Richards has left Laos, after several days spent trying to rescue miners missing in a cave.

gallery.altText: A man with a beard and cap in a jungle camp

thumbnail.altText: A man with a beard and cap in a jungle camp

items.title: 'Infuriating': Aboriginal voters turned away, told to line up again on SA election day

children.content: South Australia's Electoral Commission was aware that forcing First Nations voters to queue twice for the Voice election would be perceived as "discriminatory behaviour". Despite that, voters say it still happened and, in some cases, they were turned away.

image.altText: A woman wearing a maroon shirt and checked cardigan looks over her right shoulder.

items.title: Heatwaves are becoming so extreme, they're 'breaking the biology' of plants

children.content: Record-breaking heatwaves decimated crops in Australia this year and served as a "real wake-up call" for the urgent need to develop more heat-resistant crops and research into food security.

image.altText: a generic photo of a wheat crop

items.title: How Everest guide survived six days alone with no oxygen and a few chocolates

children.content: A few chocolates found in his pocket and an avalanche may have been what saved Dawa Sherpa from almost certain death on Mount Everest.

image.altText: Dawa Sherpa being rescued.

gallery.altText: A Nepali man in a blue snow suit

thumbnail.altText: A Nepali man in a blue snow suit

items.title: Power hang on for thrilling win over Eagles, Dockers claim record win

children.content: Port Adelaide hangs on for a thrilling win over West Coast after Logan Morris kicked seven in a Brisbane QClash win and Fremantle beat North Melbourne by 124 points to claim the biggest win in its history.

image.altText: Port Adelaide players hug and and congratulate each other after a win

items.title: Karen Read sues police for alleged misconduct over boyfriend's death

children.content: After being cleared of allegations she hit her police officer boyfriend and left him to die, Karen Read now claims agencies that investigated the incident were riddled with "bigotry, misogyny [and] systemic failures".

image.altText: Karen Read in a sky blue pant suit speaking while exasperated in front of multiple black microphones, surrounded by men.

items.title: What's driving Australia's lentil boom?

children.content: Australia's winter crop production is expected to be down this year, but lentils are bucking the trend and headed towards a record.

items.title: Woman charged with hindering police investigation after man dies at Tweed Heads

children.content: Police have arrested a man and charged a woman after a man died following an alleged assault on the New South Wales North Coast.

image.altText: A suburban street with police tape and a police van in the foreground

items.title: Volpato admits he was 'a bit too scared' to make switch from Italy

children.content: New Socceroo Cristian Volpato opens up on his decision to switch allegiance from Italy to Australia ahead of the FIFA World Cup.

image.altText: Cristian Volpato brushes his hair with left hand in training

items.title: Inflatable frogs and chicken suits: How protests are approaching Trump 2.0

children.content: Demonstrators are increasingly employing inflatable costumes and whimsy. But behind the dress up, these protesters argue it is "serious play".

items.title: Hawks forward target of racist online abuse after loss to Bulldogs

image.altText: Mabior Chol takes a mark for the Hawks during an AFL match.

items.title: The club I wanted to join was waiting in Pompeii

children.content: A newly uncovered fresco in Pompeii tells the story of women, wine and Roman hedonism. Buried beneath the spectacle lies a lesson about restraint and why some discoveries should be left to the future.

image.altText: painted murals on a wall in an excavated building in Pompeii

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: Fastest-rising women's cancer in Australia still has a low profile

children.content: The incidence of uterine cancer has doubled over the past 25 years. But it's also a disease without a public profile, which means many women aren't aware of the warning signs or the risk factors.

gallery.altText: A composite image of a baby in a trolley next to a portrait image of a woman smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A composite image of a baby in a trolley next to a portrait image of a woman smiling.

items.title: New AUKUS risk as Trump orders 'Golden Fleet' battleships

children.content: A committee of Congress wants the US Navy to explain how building "Trump-class" battleships will not delay other nuclear-powered boats, such as submarines needed for AUKUS.

image.altText: Donald Trump stands next to a posterboard showing a "Trump-class" submarine

items.title: Anthony Albanese has shown he understands the Westminster system

children.content: This week the Coalition's weakness in Question Time reminded onlookers of Albanese's command of the Westminster system, while Labor insiders expressed their anxieties about One Nation's polling surge.

image.altText: Albanese looking down in a suit while touching his ear.

items.title: Andrew made money subletting cottages on his almost rent-free estate, watchdog finds

children.content: Former prince Andrew pocketed income from cottages on the Windsor estate while living at Royal Lodge under a lease that required virtually no rent, a watchdog report has found.

items.title: Nightmare turnover leaves Wembanyama, Spurs in NBA Finals hole

children.content: Victor Wembanyama turns the ball over and misses two shots in a horror final minute as the Knicks take a 2-0 series lead back to New York.

items.title: Acid attack victim's lawyer describes trial of perpetrators as a 'sham'

children.content: Four military officers are facing trial over an acid attack on an Indonesian human rights campaigner that left him seriously disfigured. But some lawyers say the legal process is a "sham".

image.altText: Andrie Yunus Profile

items.title: US and Iran trade fire in Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The US military says it has shot down four drones Iran has launched toward the Strait of Hormuz and struck some of Tehran's coastal surveillance radar sites in response.

image.altText: A group of people paddle in water.

items.title: Patients 'chained' at Sri Lankan nursing home where 13 died in fire

children.content: A "chained" patient was among more than a dozen people who died during a fire at an unregistered nursing home in Sri Lanka, according to a staff member.

image.altText: A man looks out through bars inside a nursing home

items.title: Korda stages comeback as four Aussies advance at US Open

children.content: World No.1 Nelly Korda edges closer to the top of the leaderboard at Riviera as Grace Kim leads the way for the Australian contingent.
